name: Behalf.bot — Welfare Check
description: Operator-welfare escalation: detect when the principal has been silent past a configured threshold, then ladder through Discord ping, SMS, tier-1 contacts, broader contact list, and finally a designated family contact. Generic pattern; no live-location, duress codewords, or mode inference (those personal-security components are install-specific and live private).

enabled.description: Master toggle. False by default — welfare-check requires deliberate opt-in (requires real emergency contacts and a credible silence threshold).

silence_threshold_hours.description: How many hours of silence (no Discord activity, no Oura sync, no welfare-signal-hook fires) before the escalation ladder starts.

emergency_contacts_path.description: Absolute path to emergency-contacts.json. NOT committed — always gitignored. Use emergency-contacts.template.json as the schema reference.

webhook_url.description: Discord (or alternate channel) webhook for ops alerts. Goes silent on success; fires the escalation prompt on threshold breach.

sms_provider.description: Which SMS provider to use for the cascade. Twilio is the only fully-implemented provider in chassis V1; others are stubs. See docs/plugins/welfare-check.md (forthcoming) for the per-provider matrix.

activation._note: Plugin is dormant by default — welfare automation requires deliberate opt-in via chassis.config.yaml.modules.welfare_check.enabled = true.

_chassis_status._explanation: Welfare-check is the operator-welfare core of what was originally a larger Angel Protocol skill. The personal-security components (live location, duress codewords, mode inference, camera integration, iMessage emergency-group cascade) were intentionally moved out of the chassis to per-installer private repos — exposing the bodyguard playbook publicly would give would-be adversaries the protocol. Operators who need the bodyguard layer build it as an install-specific plugin in their private repo, alongside this welfare-check core.
